overview.tagline: An affluent, historic borough with award-winning schools and timeless small-town elegance
overview.description: Ho-Ho-Kus is one of Bergen County's most charming and affluent communities, known for its rich colonial history, top-rated public schools, and beautifully maintained homes. The borough's name derives from the Lenape word meaning 'running water,' a reference to the Ho-Ho-Kus Brook that winds through town. With a population of about 4,000, Ho-Ho-Kus offers an intimate, village-like atmosphere where neighbors know each other and community events bring everyone together, all within easy commuting distance of New York City.
highlights: Top-rated K-8 school district in Bergen County
Rich colonial and Revolutionary War history
The Ho-Ho-Kus Inn — historic landmark and fine dining destination
NJ Transit rail station on the Bergen County Line
Beautifully maintained historic homes and tree-lined streets

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Ho-Ho-Kus maintains assessed values through periodic revaluations.
realEstate.recentTrends: Ho-Ho-Kus remains a highly sought-after market driven by its schools, character, and location. Homes sell quickly, often above asking price. The mix of historic colonials and updated modern homes on generous lots keeps the market competitive.
neighborhoodNotes: Hollywood Avenue area — Grand homes and tree-lined streets, the borough's premier residential section
Warren Avenue — Walkable to downtown and the train station
North side — Larger lots bordering Ridgewood and Upper Saddle River
Ho-Ho-Kus Brook area — Scenic properties along the brook

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided weekly by the Borough of Ho-Ho-Kus.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by appointment through the DPW
Holiday schedule posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected weekly by the Borough.
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, cans, plastic) collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected separately
Recycling bins provided by the Borough
Yard waste collected seasonally
Special collection events held throughout the year

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Ridgewood Water and sewer service is managed by the Borough.
details: Water supplied by Ridgewood Water utility
Sewer service maintained by the Borough DPW
Billing is quarterly for water service
Water quality reports available through Ridgewood Water
Emergency water line repairs available 24/7

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Zoning Board
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Historic preservation considerations for designated properties

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough manages commuter parking at the train station and on-street parking regulations.
details: Commuter parking permits available for the Ho-Ho-Kus train station lot
On-street parking available throughout the borough
Overnight parking restrictions on certain streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Limited parking near the downtown commercial area

additionalServices.title: Ho-Ho-Kus Public Library
additionalServices.description: Small but well-regarded public library serving borough residents.
details: Located at 91 Warren Avenue
Children's programs, book clubs, and community events
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Free Wi-Fi and computer access

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Ho-Ho-Kus enforces noise regulations to preserve the quiet, residential character of the borough.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction noise on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted to daytime hours
Amplified music and loud gatherings subject to enforcement after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition, consistent with the borough's high standards.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained at a reasonable height
Sidewalk snow removal required within 24 hours after snowfall
Fences must comply with borough height and setback requirements
Abandoned vehicles must be removed from property
Properties must be kept in good repair and free of debris

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Ho-Ho-Kus requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with rabies proof
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Nuisance barking subject to enforcement
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking is regulated throughout the borough with attention to commuter areas and residential streets.
keyRules: Overnight parking restricted on borough streets without a permit
Snow emergency parking bans enforced — vehicles must be off-street
Train station lot requires a valid commuter permit
Commercial vehicles prohibited on residential streets overnight
Handicapped parking violations subject to fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with borough codes and regulations.
keyRules: Rental properties should be registered with the Borough
Properties must meet housing and fire safety codes
Landlords responsible for maintaining rental units
Inspections may be conducted at change of tenancy
Short-term rentals subject to local zoning regulations

other.title: Historic Preservation
other.summary: Ho-Ho-Kus values its historic character and has guidelines for preserving significant structures.
keyRules: Alterations to historic properties may require review
The Hermitage (national historic site) is protected
New construction should be sympathetic to the borough's character
Tree removal on historic properties may require additional review

publicSchools.name: Ho-Ho-Kus Public School
publicSchools.notes: Highly rated single-school district known for academic excellence, small class sizes, and strong parental involvement

privateSchools.name: Eastern Christian School
privateSchools.notes: Christian school with a campus in North Haledon; draws students from across northern NJ including Ho-Ho-Kus

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 15 minutes

whyMoveHere: Top-rated K-6 public school with exceptional test scores
Rich history including The Hermitage, a National Historic Landmark
Beautiful, well-maintained homes on generous, landscaped lots
NJ Transit Bergen County Line station for NYC commuting
Intimate community of about 4,000 with a true village atmosphere
Walking distance to Ridgewood's vibrant downtown

commuteToNYC.duration: 45-60 minutes to Hoboken, then PATH or ferry to Manhattan
commuteToNYC.trainStation: Ho-Ho-Kus Station (NJ Transit Bergen County Line)
commuteToNYC.notes: The Ho-Ho-Kus train station provides service on the Bergen County Line to Hoboken Terminal. Rush-hour express trains make the trip faster. Bus 148 offers alternative service through Ridgewood to the Port Authority Bus Terminal.

costOfLiving.summary: Ho-Ho-Kus is an affluent community with a cost of living well above state and national averages, primarily due to high home prices and property taxes.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 50% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Exceptional K-6 public school consistently rated among the best
Beautiful historic character with well-preserved homes
Train station in town for NYC commuting
Small-town atmosphere with strong community bonds
Adjacent to Ridgewood's shops, restaurants, and amenities
cons: Very high home prices and property taxes
Small borough with no significant commercial district of its own
Public school only goes through grade 6 — sends to Ridgewood for 7-12
Limited dining and shopping within borough limits
Train commute requires transfer at Hoboken for Manhattan

restaurants.name: Ho-Ho-Kus Inn & Tavern
restaurants.description: Historic inn and restaurant dating to the colonial era, offering upscale American cuisine in an elegant, historic setting. A Bergen County landmark.

restaurants.name: Nero's Grille
restaurants.description: Italian-American restaurant offering classic dishes, steaks, and a warm neighborhood atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Latour (Ridgewood)
restaurants.description: Acclaimed French restaurant in neighboring Ridgewood, a popular destination for Ho-Ho-Kus residents seeking fine dining.

parks.name: Ho-Ho-Kus Brook Park
parks.description: Scenic park along the Ho-Ho-Kus Brook with walking paths, benches, and natural beauty. A peaceful spot in the heart of the borough.

parks.name: Ho-Ho-Kus Community Park
parks.description: Borough park with playgrounds, athletic fields, tennis courts, and picnic areas.

parks.name: The Hermitage Grounds
parks.description: Grounds surrounding The Hermitage historic site, offering a tranquil setting for walking and appreciating local history.

events.name: Ho-Ho-Kus Day
events.description: Annual community celebration with food vendors, games, live music, and activities for all ages in the borough park.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual holiday tree lighting at Borough Hall with carols, refreshments, and community gathering.

events.name: Memorial Day Ceremony
events.description: Annual memorial service honoring veterans with a ceremony at the borough's war memorial.

attractions.name: The Hermitage
attractions.description: National Historic Landmark dating to the 18th century, associated with Aaron Burr and George Washington. The Gothic Revival house museum offers tours and educational programs about colonial and Revolutionary War history.

attractions.name: Ho-Ho-Kus Inn & Tavern
attractions.description: Historic inn operating since the 1790s, combining colonial architecture with fine dining. A cherished Bergen County landmark and gathering place.

attractions.name: Downtown Ridgewood
attractions.description: The vibrant, walkable downtown of neighboring Ridgewood with shops, restaurants, and cultural events is easily accessible from Ho-Ho-Kus.

shoppingAreas: Downtown Ridgewood — walkable shops and restaurants nearby
Route 17 corridor — major retail and dining

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 333 Warren Avenue, Ho-Ho-Kus, NJ 07423

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (Valley Health System)
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
